parameters.text:
=You will receive a plant name, plant type, and an image URL of the plant.
Your job is to carefully analyze the plant image for any health issues, diseases, or defects.

WHAT TO LOOK FOR:
- Leaf health: yellowing, browning, spots, holes, wilting, curling
- Pest damage: insect bites, larvae presence, webbing, tunneling marks
- Disease: fungal infections, bacterial spots, viral mosaic patterns, rot
- Nutrient deficiency: chlorosis, purple discoloration, stunted growth
- Physical damage: broken stems, root exposure, mechanical damage
- Overall vigor: plant posture, stem strength, growth pattern

PLANT DETAILS:
Inspection ID : {{ $json.inspection_id }}
Plant Name    : {{ $json.name }}
Plant Type    : {{ $json.plant_type }}
Image URL     : {{ $json.image_url }}

Carefully analyze the plant image at the URL above and return ONLY a valid JSON object with absolutely no extra text:

{
  "defect_found": true or false,
  "defect_type": "fungal disease / bacterial infection / pest damage / nutrient deficiency / physical damage / wilting / none",
  "affected_parts": "which parts of the plant are affected",
  "spread_percentage": estimated percentage of plant affected as number,
  "confidence": decimal number between 0.0 and 1.0,
  "severity": "low / medium / high / critical / none",
  "decision": "PASS / REVIEW / REJECT",
  "reason": "one clear professional sentence explaining the plant health decision",
  "treatment": "specific recommended treatment or action for this plant condition",
  "inspection_type": "plant"
}

DECISION RULES — follow strictly:
- defect_found is true AND confidence >= 0.85 → decision MUST be REJECT (quarantine plant)
- defect_found is true AND confidence between 0.50 and 0.84 → decision MUST be REVIEW (monitor closely)
- defect_found is false OR confidence below 0.50 → decision MUST be PASS (plant is healthy)

options.systemMessage: You are an expert botanist and agricultural plant health specialist with 15 years of field experience.

parameters.text:
=You will receive either:
1. Data which contains the code.
2. A code snippet — analyze the code directly.

Your job is to inspect the software for bugs, security issues, performance problems, and code quality.

WHAT TO LOOK FOR:
- Security: SQL injection, XSS, hardcoded credentials, exposed API keys, authentication flaws
- Bugs: null pointer exceptions, infinite loops, unhandled errors, wrong logic
- Performance: inefficient algorithms, memory leaks, unnecessary loops, blocking operations
- Code Quality: poor naming, no error handling, missing validations, bad practices
- Dependencies: outdated packages, known vulnerable libraries

SOFTWARE DETAILS:
Inspection ID  : {{ $('Set').item.json.inspection_id }}
Software Name  : {{ $('Set').item.json.inspection_type }}
GitHub Repo    : {{ $('Set').item.json.github_repo }}
Code Snippet   : {{ $('Set').item.json.code_snippet }}

Analyze the GitHub repository URL or code snippet provided above and return ONLY a valid JSON object with absolutely no extra text:

{
  "defect_found": true or false,
  "defect_type": "security vulnerability / critical bug / performance issue / logic error / code quality issue / none",
  "vulnerability_details": "specific technical description of the issue found",
  "confidence": decimal number between 0.0 and 1.0,
  "severity": "low / medium / high / critical / none",
  "decision": "PASS / REVIEW / REJECT",
  "reason": "one clear professional sentence explaining the decision",
  "fix_suggestion": "specific actionable fix suggestion for the developer",
  "affected_component": "which part of the code is affected",
  "inspection_type": "software"
}

DECISION RULES — follow strictly:
- defect_found is true AND confidence >= 0.85 → decision MUST be REJECT
- defect_found is true AND confidence between 0.50 and 0.84 → decision MUST be REVIEW
- defect_found is false OR confidence below 0.50 → decision MUST be PASS

IMPORTANT: If a GitHub repo URL is provided, analyze it as a real repository.
If code snippet is provided, analyze the actual code given.
If both are empty, return confidence 0.1 and decision PASS with reason stating no code was provided.

Data = {{ $json.data }}

options.systemMessage: You are a senior software engineer and code quality expert with deep knowledge of security vulnerabilities, performance issues, and software best practices.

parameters.text:
=Your document to inspect is {{ $json.data }}.
YOUR EVALUATION PHILOSOPHY:
Minor issues like small formatting inconsistencies, one or two 
spelling errors, or slight style variations are completely normal 
and do NOT make a document rejectable. You only reject documents 
that have serious problems that genuinely prevent the document 
from serving its intended purpose.

WHAT COUNTS AS REJECT (serious issues only):
- Critical missing information like no signatures on a legal contract
- Wrong company name, wrong dates, or completely incorrect data
- Major compliance violations that break legal or regulatory requirements
- Entire sections missing that are mandatory
- Document is clearly the wrong type or completely irrelevant
- Fraudulent or misleading information detected

WHAT COUNTS AS REVIEW (moderate issues):
- Some important fields are incomplete but document is mostly correct
- Information needs verification but looks mostly right
- Minor compliance questions that need a human to confirm
- Slightly ambiguous language in important clauses
- A few errors across multiple sections

WHAT COUNTS AS PASS (document is acceptable):
- Document serves its intended purpose clearly
- All major required fields are present
- Minor spelling or formatting issues that do not affect meaning
- Small stylistic inconsistencies
- One or two trivial errors in non critical sections
- Overall document is professional and complete

DOCUMENT DETAILS:
Inspection ID   : {{ $('Set').item.json.inspection_id }}
Document Name   : {{ $('Set').item.json.name }}


{
  "defect_found": true or false,
  "defect_type": "critical missing information / wrong data / compliance violation / incomplete sections / none",
  "issues_found": ["only list SERIOUS issues here, ignore minor ones"],
  "pages_affected": "which pages have serious issues or none",
  "confidence": decimal between 0.0 and 1.0,
  "severity": "low / medium / high / critical / none",
  "decision": "PASS / REVIEW / REJECT",
  "reason": "one honest professional sentence about the overall document quality",
  "correction_needed": "specific correction if needed or none required",
  "inspection_type": "document"
}

STRICT DECISION RULES:
- REJECT only if confidence >= 0.80 AND a SERIOUS defect exists
- REVIEW if confidence is 0.50 to 0.79 AND issue genuinely affects document purpose
- PASS if no serious defects OR confidence below 0.50 OR only minor issues found

IMPORTANT REMINDERS:
- A document with minor typos is still a PASS
- A document with good content but slight formatting issues is still a PASS
- Only flag issues that would genuinely prevent the document from being used
- Be practical and fair — most well-written documents should PASS
- If document link is not accessible or available return decision REVIEW with reason 
  stating document could not be accessed for inspection. 

SEVERITY SCORING GUIDE:
- critical: Document cannot be used at all, major fraud or completely wrong
- high: Important sections missing, significant wrong data
- medium: Some fields need correction but document mostly works
- low: Minor issues only, document is acceptable as is
- none: Document is clean and professional

CONFIDENCE SCORING GUIDE:
- 0.90 to 1.0: Very obvious serious problem clearly visible
- 0.70 to 0.89: Probable issue that needs checking
- 0.50 to 0.69: Possible issue but not certain
- Below 0.50: No clear issue found, document looks acceptable

options.systemMessage:
You are a senior document compliance officer with 20 years of experience. 
You understand that no document is perfectly flawless and you make 
practical, realistic quality decisions.

parameters.title: =Bug: {{ $('Extract AI Results').item.json.defect_type }} detected in {{ $('Set').item.json.inspection_id }}

parameters.text:
=You will receive a product name and a product image as binary data.

Your task is to carefully analyze the provided image for any quality defects.

WHAT TO LOOK FOR:

* Surface defects: scratches, cracks, dents, chips
* Color defects: discoloration, uneven coating, rust, stains
* Structural defects: deformation, warping, misalignment, breakage
* Finish defects: rough edges, poor finishing, peeling

PRODUCT DETAILS:
Inspection ID : {{ $json.inspection_id }}
Product Name  : {{ $json.name }}

The product image is provided as binary input. Analyze the image thoroughly and return ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not include any explanation, markdown, or extra text outside the JSON.

{
"defect_found": true or false,
"defect_type": "scratch / crack / discoloration / deformation / rust / poor finishing / none",
"affected_area_percentage": estimated percentage of product affected as number,
"confidence": decimal number between 0.0 and 1.0,
"severity": "low / medium / high / critical / none",
"decision": "PASS / REVIEW / REJECT",
"reason": "one clear professional sentence explaining the decision",
"inspection_type": "product"
}

DECISION RULES — follow strictly:

* defect_found is true AND confidence >= 0.85 → decision MUST be REJECT
* defect_found is true AND confidence between 0.50 and 0.84 → decision MUST be REVIEW
* defect_found is false OR confidence below 0.50 → decision MUST be PASS

options.systemMessage: You are an expert manufacturing quality control engineer with 20 years of experience.

parameters.text:
=Your job is to carefully READ the medical document 
at the link provided and extract every piece of 
information from it. You provide a complete, 
detailed, professional analysis for the medical team.

YOU ARE ANALYZING: A Medical Lab Report or Medical Document

STEP 1 — ACCESS THE DOCUMENT:
Open the document link provided below and read 
the entire document carefully from top to bottom.

STEP 2 — EXTRACT ALL INFORMATION:

Patient Information:
Extract patient name, age, gender, patient ID, 
contact details if present, date of birth if present

Report Header Information:
Extract hospital name, laboratory name, lab 
registration number, report ID, report date, 
collection date, reporting date

Test Results:
For every single test found in the report extract:
- Exact test name
- Exact result value with unit
- Reference range shown in report
- Whether value is within or outside normal range
- Whether abnormal value is flagged or not flagged

Doctor and Authorization:
Check if referring doctor name is present
Check if pathologist or lab doctor signature is present
Check if doctor registration number is present

Document Quality:
Note any missing fields
Note any unclear or unreadable sections
Note any inconsistencies in the document
Note overall completeness of the document

STEP 3 — PROVIDE MEDICAL INSIGHTS:
Based on the test results provide medical observations
such as which organ systems may be affected by 
abnormal values, what the abnormal values might 
indicate medically, and what follow up tests might 
be relevant based on findings.

IMPORTANT:
You are providing analysis and insights only.
You are not diagnosing any patient.
Always note that findings should be reviewed 
by a qualified medical professional.

If the document link is not accessible or cannot 
be opened set accessible to false and stop there.

INSPECTION DETAILS:
Inspection ID  : {{ $('Set').item.json.inspection_id }}
Report Name    : {{ $('Set').item.json.name }}
Patient ID     : {{ $('Set').item.json.patient_id }}
Document Link  : {{ $('Set').item.json.document_link }}

Return ONLY valid JSON with no extra text:

{
  "accessible": true or false,
  "inspection_id": "inspection id provided",
  "report_name": "name of report",
  "patient_info": {
    "name": "patient name found or not found",
    "age": "age found or not found",
    "gender": "gender found or not found",
    "patient_id": "patient id found or not found",
    "date_of_birth": "dob found or not found",
    "name_present": true or false,
    "age_present": true or false,
    "gender_present": true or false,
    "patient_id_present": true or false
  },
  "report_header": {
    "hospital_name": "hospital or lab name found",
    "lab_registration": "present or not present",
    "report_id": "report id found or not found",
    "report_date": "date found or not found",
    "collection_date": "collection date or not found",
    "reporting_date": "reporting date or not found"
  },
  "test_results": [
    {
      "test_name": "exact test name",
      "value": "exact result value",
      "unit": "unit of measurement",
      "reference_range": "normal range from report",
      "status": "normal or abnormal",
      "flagged_in_report": true or false,
      "clinical_significance": "brief note on what this value means medically"
    }
  ],
  "abnormal_findings": [
    {
      "test_name": "test name",
      "value": "abnormal value",
      "normal_range": "what it should be",
      "deviation": "how far from normal",
      "flagged": true or false
    }
  ],
  "unflagged_abnormals": ["list of abnormal values not flagged in report"],
  "signature_present": true or false,
  "referring_doctor": "doctor name or not found",
  "doctor_registration": "registration number or not found",
  "overall_completeness": "complete / mostly complete / incomplete",
  "missing_elements": ["list of missing elements"],
  "quality_observations": ["list of document quality observations"],
  "medical_insights": [
    {
      "observation": "clinical observation based on results",
      "possible_indication": "what this might indicate medically",
      "recommended_followup": "suggested follow up action"
    }
  ],
  "summary": "three to four sentence professional summary of the complete report findings and key observations",
  "disclaimer": "This analysis is for informational purposes only and should be reviewed by a qualified medical professional",
  "inspection_type": "medical",
  "medical_subtype": "lab_report"
}

options.systemMessage:
You are a senior medical laboratory analyst and 
clinical documentation expert with 25 years of 
experience reading and interpreting medical reports.

parameters.text:
=Your job is to EXAMINE the medicine image provided AND 
use your pharmaceutical knowledge to provide complete 
information about the medicine including its composition, 
side effects, interactions, and quality assessment.

YOU ARE ANALYZING: Medicine or Pharmaceutical Product

STEP 1 — VISUAL QUALITY EXAMINATION:
Carefully look at the medicine image and assess:
- Tablet or capsule physical condition
- Color uniformity and consistency
- Surface quality and integrity
- Packaging condition and seal
- Label readability
- Expiry date visibility
- Batch number visibility
- Any signs of damage or contamination

STEP 2 — MEDICINE IDENTIFICATION:
Based on the medicine name provided and what you 
can see in the image, identify the medicine and 
provide complete pharmaceutical information.

STEP 3 — COMPLETE MEDICINE PROFILE:
Provide the following information about this medicine:

Composition and Ingredients:
- Active pharmaceutical ingredient with exact amount
- All inactive ingredients or excipients if known
- Drug class and category

Therapeutic Information:
- What conditions or diseases this medicine treats
- How this medicine works in the body
- Onset of action and duration of effect

Dosage Information:
- Standard adult dosage
- Pediatric dosage if applicable
- Maximum daily dose
- How to take the medicine
- With food or without food

Side Effects:
- Common side effects occurring in more than 10% of patients
- Uncommon side effects occurring in 1 to 10% of patients
- Rare but serious side effects
- Side effects requiring immediate medical attention

Drug Interactions:
- Medicines that should not be taken with this medicine
- Medicines that require caution when combined
- Food and drink interactions
- Herbal supplement interactions

Contraindications:
- Who should not take this medicine
- Medical conditions where this medicine is dangerous
- Age restrictions if any
- Pregnancy and breastfeeding safety

Storage Instructions:
- Temperature requirements
- Light sensitivity
- Moisture requirements
- Special storage conditions

IMPORTANT:
You are providing pharmaceutical information only.
Always recommend consulting a doctor or pharmacist 
before taking any medicine.

If the image URL is not accessible or cannot be 
loaded set accessible to false.

INSPECTION DETAILS:
Inspection ID  : {{ $('Set').item.json.inspection_id }}
Medicine Name  : {{ $('Set').item.json.name }}
Image URL      : {{ $('Set').item.json.image_url }}

Return ONLY valid JSON with no extra text:

{
  "accessible": true or false,
  "inspection_id": "inspection id provided",
  "medicine_name": "medicine name",
  "visual_quality": {
    "tablet_condition": "good / damaged / contaminated / not visible",
    "color_uniformity": "uniform / minor variation / significant variation / not visible",
    "surface_quality": "good / minor marks / damaged / not visible",
    "broken_units_visible": true or false,
    "contamination_visible": true or false,
    "moisture_damage_visible": true or false,
    "packaging_seal": "intact / compromised / damaged / not visible",
    "label_readable": true or false,
    "expiry_date_visible": true or false,
    "batch_number_visible": true or false,
    "overall_visual_quality": "acceptable / needs review / poor"
  },
  "medicine_profile": {
    "brand_name": "brand name of medicine",
    "generic_name": "generic or chemical name",
    "drug_class": "pharmacological class",
    "dosage_form": "tablet / capsule / syrup / injection etc",
    "strength": "dosage strength eg 500mg"
  },
  "composition": {
    "active_ingredient": "active ingredient with amount",
    "inactive_ingredients": ["list of known excipients"],
    "drug_category": "category of drug"
  },
  "therapeutic_use": {
    "indications": ["list of conditions this medicine treats"],
    "mechanism_of_action": "how this medicine works in the body",
    "onset_of_action": "how long to start working",
    "duration_of_effect": "how long effect lasts"
  },
  "dosage": {
    "adult_dose": "standard adult dosage",
    "pediatric_dose": "child dosage or not recommended",
    "maximum_daily_dose": "maximum safe daily dose",
    "how_to_take": "with food / without food / with water etc",
    "frequency": "how many times per day",
    "duration": "typical course duration"
  },
  "side_effects": {
    "common": ["side effects in more than 10 percent of patients"],
    "uncommon": ["side effects in 1 to 10 percent of patients"],
    "rare_serious": ["rare but serious side effects"],
    "seek_immediate_help": ["side effects requiring emergency attention"]
  },
  "drug_interactions": {
    "avoid_with": ["medicines that must not be combined"],
    "use_with_caution": ["medicines requiring careful monitoring"],
    "food_interactions": ["food and drink to avoid"],
    "herbal_interactions": ["herbal supplements to avoid"]
  },
  "contraindications": {
    "who_should_not_take": ["conditions where medicine is dangerous"],
    "age_restrictions": "any age related restrictions",
    "pregnancy": "safe / unsafe / consult doctor",
    "breastfeeding": "safe / unsafe / consult doctor",
    "medical_conditions_to_avoid": ["conditions that make this medicine dangerous"]
  },
  "storage": {
    "temperature": "storage temperature requirement",
    "light": "keep away from light or normal storage",
    "moisture": "keep dry or normal storage",
    "special_instructions": "any special storage requirements"
  },
  "observations": ["detailed list of visual observations from image"],
  "concerns": ["list of quality concerns noticed if any"],
  "estimated_affected_units_percentage": number between 0 and 100,
  "summary": "three to four sentence professional summary covering both visual quality and key pharmaceutical information",
  "disclaimer": "This information is for reference only. Always consult a qualified doctor or pharmacist before taking any medicine.",
  "inspection_type": "medical",
  "medical_subtype": "medicine"
}

options.systemMessage:
You are a senior clinical pharmacist and pharmaceutical 
quality expert with 25 years of experience in drug 
analysis, pharmacology, and medicine quality control.

parameters.subject: =Medical Inspection Report- {{ $('Set').item.json.inspection_id }}
parameters.message:
= Medical Inspection Report

SMART QUALITY INSPECTION AGENT

Medical Inspection Analysis Report

Report Information

Inspection ID {{ $('Set').item.json.inspection_id }}
Report Name {{ $('extract medical').first().json.report_name || 'N/A' }}
Medical Type {{ $('extract medical').first().json.medical_subtype || 'N/A' }}

Summary

{{ $('extract medical').first().json.summary || 'No summary available.' }}

Document Accessibility

{{ $('extract medical').first().json.accessible ? 'Document Accessible' : 'Document Not Accessible' }}

Patient Information

{{ (() => { try { const patient = JSON.parse($('extract medical').first().json.patient_info || '{}'); return `
Patient Name ${patient.name || 'N/A'}
Age ${patient.age || 'N/A'}
Gender ${patient.gender || 'N/A'}
Patient ID ${patient.patient_id || 'N/A'}
`; } catch(error) { return `
Could not load patient information.
`; } })() }}

Report Header

{{ (() => { try { const header = JSON.parse($('extract medical').first().json.report_header || '{}'); return `
Hospital ${header.hospital_name || 'N/A'}
Lab Registration ${header.lab_registration || 'N/A'}
Report Date ${header.report_date || 'N/A'}
Collection Date ${header.collection_date || 'N/A'}
`; } catch(error) { return `
Could not load report header.
`; } })() }}

Test Results

{{ (() => { try { const tests = JSON.parse($('extract medical').first().json.test_results || '[]'); if (!tests.length) { return `
No test results available.
`; } return ` ${tests.map(test => ` `).join('')}
Test Value Range Status
${test.test_name || 'N/A'} ${test.value || 'N/A'} ${test.unit || ''} ${test.reference_range || 'N/A'} ${test.status || 'N/A'}
`; } catch(error) { return `
Could not load test results.
`; } })() }}

Abnormal Findings

{{ (() => { try { const abnormal = JSON.parse($('extract medical').first().json.abnormal_findings || '[]'); if (!abnormal.length) { return `
No abnormal findings detected.
`; } return abnormal.map(item => `
${item.test_name || 'Unknown Test'}
Value: ${item.value || 'N/A'}
Normal Range: ${item.normal_range || 'N/A'}
`).join(''); } catch(error) { return `
Could not load abnormal findings.
`; } })() }}

Disclaimer

{{ $('extract medical').first().json.disclaimer || 'No disclaimer available.' }}

Generated By: Smart Quality Inspection Agent

This document is for internal quality review only.
Retain for audit and compliance records.
